narrative: OMX30 opens Monday in conflicted territory. Global risk-off signals from Asia (Nikkei -4.03%) and US tech (NASDAQ -1.40%) create a -0.425% weighted correlation drag, while European bourses (DAX +0.22%, CAC +0.19%) offer modest support. Technicals reveal tension: the index trades +5.8% above 200-MA in bull regime, but MACD has crossed below signal (histogram -7.709), ADX is strong at 43.3 with bearish DI bias, and RSI 41.25 leaves room for pullback. Copper's +1.63% and gold's +0.45% are bright spots for mining/industrials. Falling VIX (-1.81%) and US yields (-61bp) suggest panic isn't imminent, but momentum is decelerating.
morningBrief: OMX30 faces a hesitant open after Asian rout and NASDAQ dump Friday. Watch the 3123 level (yesterday close); support sits at 3085 (lower Bollinger). ADX 43.3 is in strong downtrend territory despite the bull regime—expect volatility but likely weak direction. Sectors to favour: Banks (SEB, Handelsbanken) may see modest NIM relief as yields stabilize; Boliden & mining play the copper spike (+1.63%). Avoid Tech sentiment trades (Hexagon, Ericsson) until NASDAQ steadies. Industrials mixed—Volvo/Atlas Copco benefit from USD/SEK +0.09%, but oil up +0.18% adds input cost pressure. Watch 14:30 CET US Jobless Claims for any shock. Key technical: MACD below signal and histogram negative is your shorts flag; rally only above 3145 (50-MA + margin) resets bullish lean.
drivers: Asia weakness (Nikkei -4.03%) and NASDAQ selloff (-1.40%) offsetting European stability
Strong ADX 43.3 with bearish directional bias despite bull market regime
Copper surge (+1.63%) and gold strength (+0.45%) support industrials but offset by tech/NASDAQ headwinds
RSI 41.25 neutral—no mean reversion extremes but room for downside

sectorImpacts.reason: US 10Y yield fell 61bp to 4.541% but stabilizes NIM; Swedish mortgage/deposit spreads remain intact. Defensive bid in risk-off environment.
Gold +0.45% and copper +1.63% are direct revenue drivers. Boliden most leveraged to copper strength.
USD/SEK +0.09% supports export competitiveness, but oil +0.18% and risk-off sentiment weigh on input costs and demand outlook. Net negative bias.
NASDAQ -1.40% Friday and sentiment shift overshadow any yield relief. DCF values still under pressure; avoid chasing.
Despite yield decline, refinancing cost legacy and risk-off sentiment limit upside. Likely to underperform.
Oil +0.18% creates spending pressure; EUR/SEK stable does not help H&M's sourcing. Risk-off sentiment damps discretionary.
Defensive rotation in risk-off environment; counter-cyclical to equity selloff. Modest bid expected.

watchList.reason: Copper +1.63% and gold +0.45% provide direct tailwind. Only profitable copper play on OMX30.
USD/SEK +0.09% supports export pricing, but oil cost + risk-off demand concerns offset. Monitor close above 280 SEK.
Banks benefiting from yield stabilization and defensive rotation. Watch 10Y-yield support at 4.50%.
NASDAQ -1.40% and tech sentiment negative. RSI neutral but MACD crossover suggests downside room to test 60 SEK.
Oil cost pressure + risk-off discretionary rotation + no FX tailwind. Weak technical setup.

econCalendarAlert: 14:30 CET: US Initial Jobless Claims (watch for shock above 250k—potential market jolt). No major Swedish releases today. ECB speakers possible.
topRisk: US jobs data worse-than-expected could accelerate risk-off and break support at 3085, opening down-move to 3050. Alternatively, NASDAQ relief rally could spark short-covering above 3145.
technicalSummary: MACD below signal, histogram -7.709—early deterioration warning
ADX 43.3 strong but bearish DI bias—downtrend risk despite bull backdrop
RSI 41.25 neutral, no extreme—mean reversion not yet priced
52W at 87% range suggests strength but -2.8% from high leaves room
Stochastic K27.7 D24.3 oversold zone—minor bounce potential but not confirmation
